text: There is a curious tension in the air, as if something unwanted is lingering and efforts are needed to dispel it. The dialogue I hear is light yet persistent, mirroring the determination to address whatever "thing" needs removal. Entering the next room and reading the words about workshops and invention, I sense a contrast: here, the space is about creativity and renewal. I feel both slightly uneasy—wondering what must be "gotten rid of"—and strangely invigorated by the sense of ongoing creation, as if these efforts at removal and renewal are all part of a larger cycle of making space for new possibilities. My mood is thoughtful, with a hint of anticipation.
